cj.name: CJ Reim
cj.title: Managing Partner
cj.bio:
CJ co-founded Amity in 2016 with a view to partner with the world's best technology companies. CJ dedicates his time and expertise to helping entrepreneurs develop and scale their businesses. CJ currently serves on the boards of Avochato, Borderless Labs, Ditto, Isara, MaintainX, OurFirm.ai and Stationwise, in addition to managing the firm's holdings in Core, Carta and Talkdesk among others.

With over a decade of experience in the digital asset ecosystem, CJ has been at the forefront of blockchain technology. He is an Initial Contributor to Core DAO, an investor in multiple leading crypto exchanges, and was a participant in the original Genesis Block of Ethereum.

Prior to founding Amity, CJ was a Vice President at Highland Capital Partners in Palo Alto where he focused on technology investments across all stages of company growth.
cj.education: B.S. in Management with concentrations in Finance and Information Systems, Boston College
cj.bookRecommendation: A Message to Garcia by Elbert Hubbard
cj.skillDescription: Fund strategy, LP relations, enterprise/infra deals

patrick.name: Patrick Yang
patrick.title: Partner
patrick.bio:
Patrick co-founded Amity in 2016 and strives to being the most important board partner to obsessive, high agency founders.

Prior to Amity, Patrick was a Vice President at Highland Capital Partners ($8B+ AUM global venture firm). Patrick started his career at Morgan Stanley in their Menlo Park technology investment banking group.
patrick.education: B.S. in Business Administration with Engineering Leadership concentration, UC Berkeley
patrick.community: Board member of Advancing Women in Tech

andy.name: Andy Bromberg
andy.title: Partner
andy.bio: Andy joined Amity Ventures in September 2024 after more than a decade of founding and serving as CEO of venture-backed companies. He brings this operating experience and his engineering background to bear when helping founders build their businesses. He loves getting in the weeds and helping the Amity portfolio with problems big or small.
andy.education: Mathematics and Computer Science, Stanford University, where he co-founded the Stanford Bitcoin Group before dropping out to found Sidewire.
careerHighlights: Co-founded and served as founding CEO of CoinList, a digital asset platform that has facilitated token sales for major projects including Solana, Filecoin, Near, and Flow. Last valued at $1.5 billion.

Co-founded and served as CEO of Eco, building stablecoin liquidity infrastructure. Eco has raised over $90 million from leading investors including a16z Crypto, L Catterton, Activant Capital, Founders Fund, Lightspeed Venture Partners, and Pantera Capital.
Co-founded and served as CEO of Sidewire, a conversation platform that delivered expert analysis on current events.
Founding board member of FreeWorld, a non-profit seeking to end generational poverty and recidivism by providing access to living-wage jobs for people with criminal histories.
Co-founder of Lightwork Home Health, a home health assessment company, and interface0, an LLM interface for power users & teams.
andy.bookRecommendation: Seeing that Frees by Rob Burbea

andy-ravreby.name: Andy Ravreby
andy-ravreby.title: Partner
andy-ravreby.bio:
For the past 15 years, Andy has partnered with founders as both an investor and operator across AI, SaaS, and developer tools.

Most recently, he led Finance at EvenUp, an AI-native legal tech company, where he helped pioneer the "sell work, not software" model — delivering the legal outputs themselves rather than copilots for attorneys. During his tenure, he guided the company through hypergrowth from Series A to Series E, raising over $400M from Bessemer, Lightspeed, and Bain Capital.

Andy began his investment career at Citadel Global Equities, covering public technology companies. He later joined Square, contributing through its IPO, before moving into early-stage venture. In 2018, he became the first member of Amity's investment team, helping raise, deploy, and support Amity Ventures I.

Andy lives in Moraga, CA with his wife and two children.
andy-ravreby.education: Business Administration, UC Berkeley. MBA, The Wharton School.

kieran.name: Kieran Dennis
kieran.title: Investor
kieran.bio:
After joining Amity Ventures as a Summer Intern, Kieran transitioned to a full-time role with the firm.

In addition to serving on the Amity investment team, Kieran operates as an Initial Contributor at Core, an Amity-backed initiative focused on Bitcoin scaling and application development, where he works across strategy and operations.
kieran.education: B.A. in Philosophy with a minor in Finance, Boston College
kieran.interests: Praxeology, Thomism, & the New York Football Giants

peter.name: Peter Bell
peter.title: Senior Advisor
peter.bio:
Peter Bell partners with entrepreneurs to build companies of consequence. A seasoned venture capitalist and former operator, Peter serves as a Senior Advisor at San Francisco-based Amity Ventures and a General Partner at SSC Venture Partners, a firm dedicated to backing the Boston College innovation ecosystem.

Peter's career is defined by identifying and backing entrepreneurs at the forefront of paradigm shifts. After beginning his career at Price Waterhouse, he joined EMC Corporation in its early stages, relocating to Silicon Valley in 1987 to help establish the company's presence during the rise of enterprise computing. In 1998, he co-founded StorageNetworks, a pioneer in cloud computing, leading the company through its IPO as CEO in 2000.

In 2006, Peter joined Highland Capital Partners, eventually serving as Managing General Partner of the $4B global firm. His track record of partnering early with founders has resulted in six unicorn companies. Today, his investment focus targets sectors transforming how we live and work, including Artificial Intelligence, Space Tech, Cybersecurity, and Digital Health. He currently serves on the boards of Accelevents, Causely, Playco, and Prompt Health, and previously served on the board of Apex Technology Acquisition Corp during its $2B merger with AvePoint (Nasdaq: AVPT).

A committed educator and mentor, Peter has served on the faculty at both the MIT Sloan School of Management and the Carroll School of Management at Boston College. He is a Trustee Associate at Boston College and an Entrepreneur in Residence at BC's Shea Center for Entrepreneurship.
peter.education: B.S., Boston College. MBA, Harvard Business School. Honorary Doctorate, Babson College.
peter.interests: All things Boston College, Red Sox, Patriots, Celtics, and Bruins
peter.hiddenTalent: Won college airband competition performing a Billy Idol song
peter.bookRecommendation: Theo of Golden by Allen Levi

jason.name: Jason Wong
jason.title: Director of Finance & Operations
jason.bio: Jason oversees financial reporting to investors, budgeting, cash management, and partnership agreement compliance at Amity Ventures.
jason.education: B.S. in Economics, University of California, Santa Cruz. CPA licensed in California.
jason.previousRole: VP of Finance and Fund Operations at FundersClub. Tax Manager at Frank, Rimerman + Co. LLP, working with technology startups, venture capital firms, and entrepreneurs.
jason.interests: Giants, Niners, Warriors. Hawaii and tropical destinations.

colleen.name: Colleen Johnson
colleen.title: Manager of Finance & Operations
colleen.bio: Colleen manages accounting, finance, compliance, and investor relations functions at Amity Ventures.
colleen.education: B.A. in Economics and Accounting, UC Santa Barbara. Licensed CPA in California.
colleen.previousRole: Manager at EY in Strategy & Transactions and audit practices, focusing on financial services and technology sector clients.
colleen.interests: Live music, cooking, sports, and long walks with her dog Woogie the mini aussie
colleen.favoriteMeal: A spread of oysters, pickles, and champagne

christina.name: Christina Dong
christina.title: Executive Administrator
christina.bio: Christina manages executive support, administration, and communications for the Amity team. She has an educational background in journalism and English literature, with writing published in Stanford University News Service, Palo Alto Weekly, and Gentry Magazine.
christina.previousRole: Recruiting and corporate communications at Adaptive Insights (now Workday Adaptive Planning).
christina.favoriteAdventure: Exploring the Scottish highlands solo, by train (and a couple hitchhiked car rides)
